feed.title: blank
feed.subtitle: Personal website and blog of Michael Zaitsev.

entry.title: Welcome to my blog
entry.content:
Hi, and welcome to my blog. This is a placeholder first post while I get things set up. Mostly, it exists to confirm that LaTeX math renders properly.

Inline math works like this: the Pythagorean theorem is \(a^2 + b^2 = c^2\), and Euler’s identity, \(e^{i\pi} + 1 = 0\), is often called the most beautiful equation in mathematics.

Display equations work too. Here is the Gaussian integral:

\[\int_{-\infty}^{\infty} e^{-x^2} \, dx = \sqrt{\pi}\]

And the definition of the derivative:

\[f'(x) = \lim_{h \to 0} \frac{f(x + h) - f(x)}{h}\]

If the expressions above show up as typeset math rather than raw dollar-sign text, everything is working. More real content coming soon.
entry.summary: First post — and a quick test that LaTeX math renders correctly.
